channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says 25 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum coming Monday
item.description: Trump said that the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked what countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump picks Chiefs for Super Bowl win, praising Mahomes
item.description: “I guess, you have to say that when a quarterback wins as much as he’s won, I have to go with Kansas City,” Trump told Fox News’s Bret Baier.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump starts term with positive approval rating in CBS poll
item.description: In the CBS/YouGov poll, 53 percent said that when it comes to “the way Donald Trump is handling his job as president,” they support it.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: US Marine from California identified as one of 4 people killed in Philippines plane crash
item.description: A U.S. Marine from California has been identified by military officials as one of four people killed when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colorado deputy takes out armed man during ‘active shooter’ call at family entertainment center
item.description: A sheriff’s deputy in Colorado shot and killed a man wielding a firearm during an “active shooter" call at a family entertainment center on Saturday night, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Putting carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where it won’t contribute to global warming could see less federal support and enthusiasm under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stage and film actor Tony Roberts dies at 85
item.description: Tony Roberts, a versatile, Tony Award-nominated theater performer at home in both plays and musicals and who appeared in several Woody Allen movies — often as Allen's best friend — has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Squatter turns couple's dream home purchase into nightmare
item.description: A New York couple has been unable to move into their new Rochester home because a squatter has taken over and refused to leave, even after payment offers to get him out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As female representation hits new highs among states, constitutions still assume officials are male
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — South Dakota’s constitution refers to the governor as a “he.” But for the past six years, the governor was a “she” — Kristi Noem, the first female to hold the position there. The conservative Republican pushed a constitutional amendment to change the gendered language.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Dozens more Alexander brother accusers come forward as real estate tycoons plead not guilty
item.description: Luxury real estate brokers Tal and Oren Alexander and their brother Alon pleaded not guilty to sex trafficking charges, and prosecutors say at least 60 victims have come forward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Survivor of 1982 DC plane crash credits pilot school lesson with saving his life
item.description: After a D.C. plane collision Jan. 29, Joseph Stiley, survivor of an eerily similar 1982 plane crash in D.C., remembers how he fought to survive in the freezing Potomac.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump deporting criminal aliens to Guantanamo Bay: Meet the hardened terrorists they'll join
item.description: Meet the terrorist prisoners at Guantánamo Bay that the illegal criminal migrants will join following President Donald Trump's executive order to use the infamous prison to house migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: A hidden gem of whitewater rafting faces an uncertain future after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ERWIN, Tenn. (AP) — After 24 years of guiding whitewater trips on the Nolichucky River Gorge for other companies, Patrick Mannion finally received a permit last year to operate his own outfitter business.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board were dead, authorities said. The crash was one of the deadliest in the state in the last 25 years.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Woman is accused of stealing from a TV reporter found dead at his hotel before the Super Bowl
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Police are investigating the death of a television reporter who was in New Orleans to cover the Super Bowl and on Friday announced the arrest of a woman they say used his credit card.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A mother and daughter were killed in tornadoes that struck eastern Tennessee
item.description: WARTBURG, Tenn. (AP) — Two tornadoes moved through eastern Tennessee, leaving a mother and daughter dead and injuring three other people, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Red states pursue their own DOGE-style reforms
item.description: Numerous Republican-led states are seeking to implement measures to slash the size and cost of state governments in an effort to mirror the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, ABC 4

item.title: Gatorade colors, on-field proposals and other bets before Super Bowl
item.description: Known as prop bets, the annual list of side wagers includes game-related stats as well as creative propositions.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates vow to fight for the rights of NYC immigrants
item.description: Some migrants in New York City are staying away from school, church, and work – to avoid arrest during an ICE immigration raid.
item.dc:creator: Jay Dow, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE staffer who accessed Treasury Department payment systems resigns after racist posts exposed
item.description: “Normalize Indian hate,” the account associated with Marko Elez posted in September, regarding people of Indian ethnicity who work in the U.S. tech sector, the Wall Street Journal reported.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Chris Swanson, prominent sheriff and Democrat, announces run for Michigan governor
item.description: FLINT, Mich. (AP) — Chris Swanson, the high-profile sheriff of Michigan's Genesee County, is launching a Democratic bid to become the battleground state's next governor.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends bipartisan National Prayer Breakfast, a decadeslong Washington tradition
item.description: President Donald Trump participated in the National Prayer Breakfast at the Capitol on Thursday, joining a more than 70-year-old Washington tradition that brings together a bipartisan group of lawmakers for fellowship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Protesters in cities across the US rally against Trump's policies, Project 2025 and Elon Musk
item.description: Demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. on Wednesday to protest the Trump administration's early actions, decrying everything from the president's immigration crackdown to his rollback of transgender rights and a proposal to forcibly transfer Palestinians from the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blake Lively is sued by Texas crisis specialist in latest 'It Ends With Us' lawsuit
item.description: A Texas crisis communications specialist has sued Blake Lively for defamation after the actor pulled him into her legal fight with co-star and director Justin Baldoni over their film, “It Ends With Us.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A Texas man is executed for the killing of a pastor during a robbery at a church
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his church during a robbery was put to death Wednesday evening, the second execution in the U.S. this year and the first of four scheduled in Texas over the next three months.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
